text:
You did it. Twenty-six point two miles, start to finish. That is not a small thing — it is one of the harder physical challenges a person can take on, and you completed it.

Your legs probably feel it. Your feet definitely do. Rest them. You earned that.

Most people never attempt a marathon. You trained, showed up on race day, and crossed the line. Whatever your time was, the distance was the same as everyone else's.

Congratulations. That accomplishment belongs to you now, permanently. No one can take it back.

What's next?
